turns.content:
Thinking
The user wants a weeks burndown chart. Let me first check what tools are available for this, particularly the burndown renderer.
Let me get the issue data and render a burndown chart for you.
✅ Check burndown tool usage · 1.1s
Bash(command="python tools/render/burndown.py --help 2>&1", description="Check burndown tool usage")
usage: burndown.py [-h] [--start START] [--end END] [--exclude EXCLUDE]
                   [--port PORT]

Render an issue burndown bar chart to the dashboard

options:
  -h, --help         show this help message and exit
  --start START      Start date YYYY-MM-DD (default: 7 days ago)
  --end END          End date YYYY-MM-DD (default: today)
  --exclude EXCLUDE  Comma-separated issue numbers to exclude (e.g.
                     "48,49,111")
  --port PORT
✅ Check burndown tool usage · 0.6s
Bash(command="python tools/render/burndown.py --help 2>&1", description="Check burndown tool usage")
usage: list_issues.py [-h] [--state {open,closed,all}] [--limit LIMIT]
                      [--label LABEL] [--repo REPO]

List GitHub issues

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --state {open,closed,all}
  --limit LIMIT
  --label LABEL
  --repo REPO
Thinking
The burndown tool defaults to 7 days ago to today. The user said "weeks burndown" - I think they want a weekly burndown. Let me run it with the default settings first, which covers the last week. Or maybe they want multiple weeks? Let me just run the default burndown which covers the last 7 days (1 week). Actually, "weeks burndown" might mean they want a burndown spanning several weeks. Let me set the start to maybe 4 weeks ago to give a broader view.
✅ Render 4-week burndown chart to dashboard · 1.6s
Bash(command="python tools/render/burndown.py --start 2026-04-06 --end 2026-05-04 2>&1", description="Render 4-week burndown chart to dashboard")

=== BURNDOWN BAR ===
Range: 2026-04-06 to 2026-05-04 | Excluded: 0
| Closed: 91 | Still Open: 13
Open in dashboard
Download PNG

Here's your 4-week burndown (Apr 6 → May 4): - **91 issues closed** over the period - **13 still open** [Open in dashboard](http://127.0.0.1:8421/public/charts/043ad3b2235c.html) · [Download PNG](http://127.0.0.1:8421/renderpng?src=/public/charts/043ad3b2235c.html)
